sync.c(3) Library Functions Manual sync.c(3)

NAME

sync.c - 🔒 Synchronization primitive debugging with dynamic state inspection

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct sync_buffer_t
struct debug_state_request_t

Macros


#define SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE 65536

Enumerations


enum debug_request_type_t { DEBUG_REQUEST_STATE, DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE, DEBUG_REQUEST_MEMORY_REPORT }

Functions


void debug_sync_print_state (void)
void debug_sync_final_cleanup (void)
Final cleanup of all debug allocations at shutdown. void debug_sync_print_state_delayed (uint64_t delay_ns)
Schedule delayed debug state printing on debug thread. void debug_sync_print_backtrace_delayed (uint64_t delay_ns)
Schedule delayed backtrace printing on debug thread. void debug_sync_set_memory_report_interval (uint64_t interval_ns)
Set periodic memory report interval. void debug_sync_set_main_thread_id (void)
int debug_sync_init (void)
uint64_t debug_sync_get_main_thread_id (void)
bool debug_sync_is_cleanup_in_progress (void)
int debug_sync_start_thread (void)
void debug_sync_destroy (void)
void debug_sync_cleanup_thread (void)
void debug_sync_trigger_print (void)
void debug_sync_get_stats (uint64_t *total_acquired, uint64_t *total_released, uint32_t *currently_held)
int debug_sync_mutex_lock (mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_mutex_trylock (mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_mutex_unlock (mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_rdlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_rdunlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_wrlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_wrunlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_wait (cond_t *cond, mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_timedwait (cond_t *cond, mutex_t *mutex, uint64_t timeout_ns, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_signal (cond_t *cond, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_broadcast (cond_t *cond, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
bool debug_sync_is_initialized (void)

Detailed Description

🔒 Synchronization primitive debugging with dynamic state inspection

Date

February 2026

Clean architecture: queries named.c registry for current state. No internal collection overhead - inspects lock structs directly.

Definition in file sync.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE 65536

Referenced by debug_sync_print_state().

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum debug_request_type_t

Enumerator

DEBUG_REQUEST_STATE
DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE
DEBUG_REQUEST_MEMORY_REPORT

Definition at line 428 of file sync.c.

428              {
429   DEBUG_REQUEST_STATE,         // Print sync state
430   DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE,     // Print backtrace
431   DEBUG_REQUEST_MEMORY_REPORT, // Print memory report
432 } debug_request_type_t;










Function
  Documentation




void
  debug_sync_cleanup_thread (void )


Definition at line 667 of file sync.c.


667                                      {
668   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Starting cleanup");
669 
670   // Only join if thread was actually created
671   if (!g_debug_state_request.initialized) {
672     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread not initialized, returning");
673     return;
674   }
675 
676   // Set cleanup flag to prevent deadlock checks from accessing freed memory
677   // Do this after checking initialization so we don't set it unnecessarily
678   atomic_store_bool(&g_cleanup_in_progress, true);
679   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread was initialized, proceeding with cleanup");
680 
681   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Setting initialized to false");
682   g_debug_state_request.initialized = false; // Prevent double-join
683 
684   // Signal the thread to wake up immediately instead of waiting for 100ms timeout
685   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Signaling thread to exit");
686   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.should_exit, true);
687   cond_signal(&g_debug_state_request.cond);
688   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Signal sent, about to join thread");
689 
690   // Use a timeout join to ensure we don't deadlock, but still unregister the thread
691   // The debug thread should exit quickly after should_exit is set above
692   int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_debug_thread, NULL, 1000000000ULL); // 1 second timeout
693   if (join_result == 0) {
694     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread joined successfully");
695   } else if (join_result == -2) {
696     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread join timed out (thread may still be running)");
697     // Don't unregister if timeout - thread is still alive
698   } else {
699     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread join failed with error %d", join_result);
700   }
701 }






References atomic_store_bool().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy(),
    debug_sync_destroy(), and session_client_like_run().

int
  debug_sync_cond_timedwait (cond_t * cond, mutex_t * mutex, uint64_t
  timeout_ns, const char * file_name, int line_number, const char *
  function_name)


Definition at line 790 of file sync.c.


791                                                          {
792   // Same as debug_sync_cond_wait(): skip tracking the atomic unlock/relock.
793   (void)file_name;
794   (void)line_number;
795   (void)function_name;
796   return cond_timedwait_impl(cond, mutex, timeout_ns);
797 }






References mutex.






int
  debug_sync_cond_wait (cond_t * cond, mutex_t * mutex, const char * file_name,
  int line_number, const char * function_name)


Definition at line 779 of file sync.c.


780                                                     {
781   // Note: pthread_cond_wait() atomically releases and re-acquires the mutex,
782   // but this happens at the kernel level. Debug tracking can't monitor this atomic
783   // operation properly, so we skip cond_on_wait() to avoid false deadlock reports.
784   (void)file_name;
785   (void)line_number;
786   (void)function_name;
787   return cond_wait_impl(cond, mutex);
788 }






References mutex.

void
  debug_sync_print_backtrace_delayed (uint64_t delay_ns)


Schedule delayed backtrace printing on debug thread.


Parameters


delay_ns Nanoseconds to sleep before
  printing


Definition at line 610 of file sync.c.


610                                                            {
611   mutex_lock(&g_debug_state_request.mutex);
612   g_debug_state_request.request_type = DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE;
613   g_debug_state_request.delay_ns = delay_ns;
614   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.should_run, true);
615   cond_signal(&g_debug_state_request.cond);
616   mutex_unlock(&g_debug_state_request.mutex);
617 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
    DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE.






void
  debug_sync_print_state (void )


Definition at line 380 of file sync.c.


380                                   {
381 // Use a single large buffer for all sync state output
382 #define SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE 65536  // Increased from 8192 to handle many syncs
383   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] ENTRY");
384 
385   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE, char *);
386   if (!buffer) {
387     log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Failed to allocate buffer");
388     return;
389   }
390 
391   sync_buffer_t buf = {.buffer = buffer, .buffer_size = SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE, .offset = 0};
392 
393   // Iterate through all registered syncs
394   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Iterating mutexes");
395   named_registry_for_each(mutex_iter_callback, &buf);
396   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Iterating rwlocks");
397   named_registry_for_each(rwlock_iter_callback, &buf);
398   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Iterating conds");
399   named_registry_for_each(cond_iter_callback, &buf);
400 
401   named_registry_for_each(atomic_t_iter_callback, &buf);
402   named_registry_for_each(atomic_ptr_iter_callback, &buf);
403 
404   // Print lock stacks for deadlock analysis
405   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Getting lock stacks");
406   debug_sync_print_lock_stacks(buf.buffer, buf.buffer_size, &buf.offset);
407 
408   // Log everything in one call
409   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Buffer size: %zu bytes", buf.offset);
410   if (buf.offset > 0) {
411     log_info("SYNC_STATE:\n%s", buf.buffer);
412   } else {
413     log_info("SYNC_STATE: (empty)");
414   }
415 
416   SAFE_FREE(buffer);
417 #undef SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE
418 }






References sync_buffer_t::buffer,
    sync_buffer_t::buffer_size, named_registry_for_each(),
    sync_buffer_t::offset, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE.


Referenced by crypto_handshake_init().

void
  debug_sync_set_memory_report_interval (uint64_t interval_ns)


Set periodic memory report interval.


Parameters


interval_ns Interval in nanoseconds (0 to
  disable)


Definition at line 623 of file sync.c.

624   g_debug_state_request.memory_report_interval_ns = interval_ns;

int
  debug_sync_start_thread (void )


Definition at line 650 of file sync.c.


650                                   {
651   // Initialize mutex and condition variable for signal wakeup
652   if (!g_debug_state_request.initialized) {
653     mutex_init(&g_debug_state_request.mutex, "debug_sync_state");
654     cond_init(&g_debug_state_request.cond, "debug_sync_signal");
655     g_debug_state_request.initialized = true;
656   }
657 
658   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.should_exit, false);
659   int err = asciichat_thread_create(&g_debug_thread, "debug_sync", debug_print_thread_fn, NULL);
660   return err;
661 }






References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), and mutex_init().


Referenced by main().






void
  debug_sync_trigger_print (void )


Definition at line 703 of file sync.c.


703                                     {
704   // Set flag to trigger printing on debug thread (from SIGUSR1 handler).
705   // We don't call debug_sync_print_state() directly here to avoid logging
706   // in signal handler context, which could deadlock with logging mutexes.
707   // Uses atomic_store for thread-safe flag setting from signal handler.
708   //
709   // Signal the condition variable to wake up the debug thread immediately
710   // (without waiting for the 100ms timeout).
711   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.signal_triggered, true);
712   cond_signal(&g_debug_state_request.cond);
713 }






References atomic_store_bool().


Referenced by session_handle_keyboard_input().
